summary: Reordered the public `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` signature so the optional `Fighter_GObj* arg3` parameter moved from immediately after `MotionFlags flags` to the final argument position, after `anim_start`, `anim_speed`, and `anim_blend`. The PR mechanically updated roughly 160 fighter-related source/header files and included a regex migration script in the PR text. Evidence suggests this was a decomp-matching cleanup/API normalization pass rather than a gameplay logic change; the body excerpt includes a matching report for `ftCo_Shouldered.c` and the diff is dominated by call-site argument order changes.
what_changed: `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` declaration and definition changed from `(gobj, msid, flags, Fighter_GObj* arg3, float anim_start, float anim_speed, float anim_blend)` to `(gobj, msid, flags, float anim_start, float anim_speed, float anim_blend, Fighter_GObj* arg3)`.
All visible call sites were updated to move the old fourth argument, commonly `NULL`, to the final argument position.
Typical call-site pattern changed from `Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, msid, flags, NULL, 0, 1, 0)` to `Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, msid, flags, 0, 1, 0, NULL)`.
Call sites preserving the current animation frame were similarly rewritten, e.g. `NULL, fp->cur_anim_frame, 1, 0` became `fp->cur_anim_frame, 1, 0, NULL`.
The update touched broad fighter code: common states such as attacks, cliff, damage, guard, item/lift/cargo, character special moves, Master Hand states, and top-level fighter helper files.

The PR text included a Python regex script that recursively rewrote `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` calls under `src`, mapping captured groups as old argument 4 to new argument 7 and shifting arguments 5-7 left.
smart_moves: Kept the refactor narrowly scoped: the function name and behavior-facing call sites stayed the same while only parameter ordering changed.
Updated `fighter.c` and `fighter.h` together, preventing prototype/definition drift after the signature reorder.
Used a scripted migration for a large, repetitive seven-argument call pattern, reducing manual editing risk across 160 changed files.
The regex script preserved multiline calls well enough to cover the many formatted `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` invocations shown in the diff.

Grouped the animation parameters together in the signature (`anim_start`, `anim_speed`, `anim_blend`) and left the optional object pointer at the end, making call sites visually clearer when the pointer is usually `NULL`.
decomp_lessons: For high-fanout fighter APIs, even a small prototype-order correction requires updating every call site in the same PR to keep matching and typechecking coherent.
When a function has many calls with mostly constant arguments, include the exact migration script in the PR body; it makes the mechanical nature of the diff auditable.
Argument order can matter for decomp matching even when semantic values are unchanged, especially around mixed pointer/integer/float parameters in a heavily called low-level routine.
After a mechanical signature refactor, check representative matching reports in files with known mismatches; this PR surfaced a `ftCo_Shouldered.c` report in the PR text.
Keep mechanical API churn separate from logic changes so reviewers and future decomp agents can distinguish matching-oriented call-shape fixes from behavior edits.
naming_conventions: `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` name was retained; the PR did not introduce a new wrapper or alternate naming scheme.
The reordered prototype explicitly names the float animation arguments together as `anim_start`, `anim_speed`, and `anim_blend`.
The pointer parameter remained named `arg3`, indicating its exact semantic name was still uncertain in this slice despite its type being `Fighter_GObj*`.
Call sites continue to use existing motion state enum/name conventions such as `ftCa_MS_SpecialHi`, `ftCo_MS_Attack11`, and `Ft_MF_None`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use prototype argument order as a matching lever: reorder the C signature to better match the target ABI/codegen expectations, then migrate all calls consistently.
For a seven-argument function with frequent `NULL, 0, 1, 0` tails, a capture-group regex can safely express the intended transformation when the function name and arity are stable.
Representative before/after pattern: old `(flags, NULL, 0, 1, 0)` became new `(flags, 0, 1, 0, NULL)`.
Preserve nonconstant animation-frame arguments during mechanical migration, e.g. `fp->cur_anim_frame` stayed as the animation-start argument after the pointer moved to the end.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
Because there was no reviewer discussion in the slice, inferred intent is limited to the title, body script, matching report, and diff.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` calls with old order pattern `, NULL, 0, 1, 0` after PR 902.
Investigate the semantic role of `Fighter_GObj* arg3` in `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` and whether it can be renamed.
Compare `ftCo_Shouldered.c` match scores before and after this signature reorder.
Find all non-NULL final-argument uses of `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` to understand the moved pointer parameter.
Check later PRs for additional cleanup around `anim_start`, `anim_speed`, `anim_blend`, or motion state API wrappers.
